summary: Large decomp-matching PR centered on src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c. The decomp.dev report says it produced 20 new matches and +15784 matched bytes, raising matched code by +0.41%. Most gains were CPU/common fighter AI routines in ftCo_0A01, with supporting struct/prototype fixes in fighter, mp island, mp lib, hookshot item, and CPU command script headers.
what_changed: Replaced many ftCo_0A01 placeholder declarations with C implementations, including matched functions ftCo_800A101C, ftCo_800A1CC4, ftCo_800A3234, ftCo_800AA42C, ftCo_800AA844, ftCo_800AACD0, ftCo_800AAF48, ftCo_800AC7D4, ftCo_800ACB44, ftCo_800ACD5C, ftCo_800AD54C, ftCo_800AD7FC, ftCo_800ADC28, ftCo_800AFE3C, ftCo_800B00F8, ftCo_800B101C, and ftCo_800B126C.

Improved but did not fully match several large ftCo_0A01 routines according to the bot report: ftCo_800AB224 to 99.99%, ftCo_800A9CB4 to 99.30%, ftCo_800AF78C to 97.76%, ftCo_800A9904 to 98.80%, ftCo_800B1478 to 98.83%, and ftCo_800AC5A0 to 99.88%.
Added a local ftCo_803C6594_t struct in ftCo_0A01.c and changed ftCo_803C6594 from StageBlastZone* to ftCo_803C6594_t* to model the linked data used by ftCo_800A1CC4.

Corrected mp_UnkStruct0 and mp_UnkStruct5 endpoint fields from separate scalar/int slots into Vec3 x8 and Vec3 x14 fields, then updated ftCo_0A01.c and itlinkhookshot.c references to use x8.x, x8.y, x14.x, and x14.y.
Introduced struct mpIsland_80458E88_t with S32Vec3 endpoint fields for the global mpIsland_80458E88, while regular island entries use Vec3 in mp_UnkStruct0/mp_UnkStruct5.

Improved mpIsland routines: mpIsland_8005A6F8 now zeroes named vector components, mpIsland_8005AC8C now checks mpLib_804D64C0_t flags instead of raw pointer arithmetic, and mpIsland_8005ACE8 copies Vec3 endpoints directly and walks typed mpLib data.

Updated mpLib prototypes, including mpLib_8004F008 to take int* and Vec3* output arguments, mpLib_8004F8A4 to return bool with typed Vec3/float parameters, and mpLib_800509B8 to take a Vec3* output where it previously used UNK_T.

Updated ftcmdscript function parameter names and prototypes around CPU command script writes; ftcmdscript.h added cmd_t and arg_t aliases, with M2CTX using CPUCommand/s8 and normal builds using u8/u8.

Refined Fighter_x1A88_t and Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t layout: named stick fields, reduced xFC element size to 0x1C, made xFC an array of 30 entries, typed x4C as Item*, split several padding regions into fields, and adjusted bitfields such as xF8_b12.
Added specific ftcpuattack.h prototypes for ftCo_800B9704(Fighter*), ftCo_800BA2E8(Fighter*, Fighter*) returning bool, and ftCo_800BA674(Fighter*, Fighter*).
smart_moves: The PR attacked ftCo_0A01 matching by first improving surrounding type information: mp island endpoints, fighter CPU-state buffers, mpLib signatures, and CPU command script argument types were all tightened so the main fighter code could use natural Vec3, Item*, and command enum forms.

Using Vec3 x8/x14 in mp island structs eliminated scattered scalar field guesses such as x8/xC/x10 and x14/x18/x1C, making geometry call sites in ftCo_0A01 and itlinkhookshot line up with actual coordinate semantics.

The M2CTX-only cmd_t/arg_t aliases in ftcmdscript.h preserved richer enum/signed argument information for context generation without forcing normal compiled code away from byte-sized CPU command buffers.

Small static inline helpers and explicit dontinline wrappers were used as matching tools: examples include ftCo_800A0508_dontinline, ftCo_800A20A0_dontinline, ftCo_800A5294_dontinline, ftCo_800A80E4_dontinline, is_small, is_capsule_egg_ball, and several function-specific inline helpers.
Typed access to mpLib_804D64C0_t flags and x4 arrays replaced raw address arithmetic in mpIsland_8005AC8C/mpIsland_8005ACE8, improving both readability and matching viability.

The ftCo_800A101C implementation carefully initializes the Fighter_x1A88_t CPU/AI state block, including Nana-specific handling, xFC ring/history entries, stick/trigger fields, random timer fields, jump height estimate, and character-specific x56C values.
decomp_lessons: For large common fighter AI files, matching often depends on adjacent struct corrections rather than only local C control flow. Here, ft/types.h and mp/types.h layout fixes unlocked many ftCo_0A01 functions.

When unknown coordinate triples appear as repeated x/y/z scalar groups, test whether they are actually Vec3 or S32Vec3; changing mp_UnkStruct0/mp_UnkStruct5 to Vec3 simplified several geometry routines.
Use M2CTX guards when richer decompiler-facing enum or signed types would help context generation but the compiled ABI/storage must remain u8-sized.

Do not leave mpLib and ftcpuattack dependencies as UNK_PARAMS if call sites reveal stable signatures. Precise prototypes reduce casts, improve stack/register allocation, and can enable matching in unrelated files.

For matching, narrowly scoped inline helpers can document repeated decisions while preserving generated code shape; however, this PR provides no reviewer discussion, so exact intent for each helper is inferred from the diff only.
PAD_STACK markers and explicit temporary variables remained necessary in several matched routines, especially ftCo_800A101C, ftCo_800A1CC4, ftCo_800A3234, and ftCo_800A9904.
Struct static asserts are important after replacing large padding with typed members: the PR kept/asserted sizes such as Fighter_x1A88_xFC_t == 0x1C and mpLib_804D64B8_t == 0x18.
naming_conventions: CPU command enum values use CpuCmd_* names such as CpuCmd_Done, CpuCmd_PressY, CpuCmd_ReleaseY, CpuCmd_LstickTowardDestination, and CpuCmd_WaitFor.
Temporary matching helpers in ftCo_0A01 use function-scoped names like ftCo_800A1CC4_inline0, ftCo_800A1CC4_inline1, ftCo_800AD54C_inline0, and ftCo_800B101C_inline0.

Explicit anti-inlining wrappers use the suffix _dontinline, for example ftCo_800A0508_dontinline, ftCo_800A20A0_dontinline, ftCo_800A5294_dontinline, ftCo_800A80E4_dontinline, and ftCo_800B0760_dontinline.
Small semantic predicates were named in plain English where known: is_small, isFacingDestination, isInTeeter, is_capsule_egg_ball, and isPopoUpB.
Newly typed but still unknown structs retain project-style address/offset naming, such as ftCo_803C6594_t, mpIsland_80458E88_t, mp_UnkStruct0, and Fighter_x1A88_t.
Fighter CPU input history fields were promoted from x6/x7/x8/x9 to lstickX, lstickY, cstickX, and cstickY.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK calls to preserve stack layout in newly decompiled routines.
Used static inline helper factoring to force or preserve specific code shape in complex condition trees.
Added _dontinline wrappers around existing functions to control call generation/inlining behavior for matching.
Adjusted function prototypes to exact pointer/value forms so call sequences match, especially ftCo_800A0FB0/mpLib_8004F008 and mpLib collision helpers.
Replaced fake/raw pointer arithmetic in mpIsland_8005AC8C with typed mpLib_804D64C0_t access while still preserving the matched flag test.
Used direct struct assignment for Vec3 endpoint copies in mpIsland_8005ACE8, a cleaner expression that also matched better than manual int stores.
Used small floating-point epsilon predicate is_small around gravity and velocity calculations in ftCo_800A9904 and ftCo_800A101C-like logic.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report, which recorded +0.41% matched code, +15784 bytes, 20 new matches, and 8 unmatched-function improvements.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type Fighter_x1A88_t fields introduced here.
Search for future cleanup of ftCo_803C6594_t and whether it moves from local ftCo_0A01.c typedef into a shared header.
Check later mp island PRs for final names of mp_UnkStruct0, mp_UnkStruct5, and mpIsland_80458E88_t.
Look for follow-up matches for ftCo_800AB224, ftCo_800A9CB4, ftCo_800AF78C, ftCo_800A9904, ftCo_800B1478, and ftCo_800AC5A0 after this near-match PR.
Search for use of ftcmdscript cmd_t/arg_t M2CTX pattern in other command-buffer headers.
